name: YouTube Audio
overview: Download YouTube audio, tag it, and push to your Jellyfin music library.
description: YouTube Audio lets you download YouTube audio (single videos or playlists) as Opus files, review and edit metadata in a staging area, then push files into your Jellyfin music library. Uses yt-dlp for downloading and TagLibSharp for Vorbis Comment tagging.

versions.changelog:
## Chores
- Fix missing poster
- Comment cleanup
- Reorganize models

## Fixes
- Fix manifest checksum always resolving to Not Found
- Fix yt-dlp auto-download failing in Docker (download standalone binary instead of Python script)

## Features
- Auto-download yt-dlp binary on first use
- Batch delete for queue and downloads
- Album artist metadata support
- Bulk album search filtered by artist
- Save button highlights yellow when row has unsaved changes

## Fixes
- Clean YouTube titles in filenames and metadata on import
- Repair corrupt Opus files with ffmpeg before import
- Prevent info.json from overwriting user-edited metadata
- Fix combo-box memory leak on document click listeners
- Fix process resource leak in ffmpeg repair
- Fix duplicate file handling on import
- Fix stale checkbox rendering on import page

## Improvements
- Settings UI matches standard Jellyfin patterns (checkboxes, dropdowns, reset buttons)
- Shared getTabs across all pages
- Moved request models to dedicated files
- Proper return types on API endpoints
- Redesigned import rows to compact 4-line card layout
- Consistent input styling across import rows and bulk edit bar
- Consistent label styling matching standard Jellyfin patterns
- Bulk edit bar always visible with Apply disabled when nothing selected
- Import and Delete buttons hidden until items are selected
- Download status text moved above search controls
